templates.name: default
templates.title: Blank Starter
templates.description: A minimal starter with a dashboard, example collection, and seed script.
templates.starter_prompt:
Build me a Lumera app for [describe your domain, e.g. "tracking customer orders", "managing support tickets", "expense approvals"]. Here is what I need:

1. **Data model**: Replace the `example_items` collection with collections for my domain with the right fields, types, and indexes.

2. **Dashboard**: Wire up the index page with real stats from my collections. Show 3-4 stat cards at the top and a recent-items table below.

3. **List page**: A page that lists records from my main collection with status filter tabs, search, and pagination. Each row should link to a detail page.

4. **Detail page**: A detail page showing a single record with an edit form, status actions (approve/reject/archive), and any related records.

5. **Seed data**: A seed script that creates realistic demo records across all collections so I can test immediately.

6. **Automation** (if needed): Background processing for any AI extraction, calculations, or notifications, triggered automatically on record creation.

templates.name: invoice-processing
templates.title: Invoice Processing
templates.description: Upload invoices, extract data with AI, review and approve.
templates.starter_prompt:
Build me an invoice processing app. I want to upload invoice documents (PDF, images), have AI automatically extract the key fields (vendor name, invoice number, dates, amounts, line items), then review and approve them.

Here is what I need:

1. **Upload and extract**: A way to upload an invoice document. When uploaded, an AI automation should read the document and extract structured data — vendor, invoice number, invoice date, due date, total amount, currency, and line items. The extracted fields should populate the invoice record for review.

2. **Review workflow**: Invoices should flow through statuses: draft → processing → review → approved or rejected. After AI extraction, the invoice lands in "review" where I can edit any fields the AI got wrong, then approve or reject.

3. **Dashboard**: A dashboard showing total invoices, how many are pending review, how many approved, and how many in draft. Plus a table of recent invoices.

4. **Invoice list**: A list page with status filter tabs, pagination, and links to each invoice detail page.

5. **Invoice detail**: A detail page showing the uploaded document side-by-side with the extracted fields in an editable form. Approve and reject buttons at the top.

6. **Vendor and GL management**: Collections for vendors (with default GL codes) and GL accounts. A settings page to manage them.

7. **Audit log**: Track all creates, updates, and deletes across invoices, vendors, and GL accounts with before/after state.

8. **Seed data**: A seed script with sample vendors, GL accounts, and a few demo invoices.

templates.name: payroll-journal-entry
templates.title: Payroll Journal Entry
templates.description: Upload payroll reports, extract data with AI, review and post journal entries.
templates.starter_prompt:
Build me a payroll journal entry app. I want to upload payroll reports from my payroll provider (PDF or CSV), have AI read the report and generate the debit/credit journal entry lines, review them for accuracy, and then post.

Here is what I need:

1. **Upload and extract**: A way to upload a payroll report. When uploaded, an AI automation should read the document — whether it's a PDF or CSV — and generate journal entry lines with account codes, account names, departments, debit amounts, credit amounts, and memos.

2. **Review workflow**: Payroll runs should flow through statuses: draft → processing → review → posted or rejected. After extraction, the run lands in "review" with all journal lines displayed. Flag it if debits don't equal credits.

3. **Dashboard**: A dashboard showing total payroll runs, how many are pending review, how many posted, and how many in draft. Plus a table of recent runs showing pay period, pay date, total debits, and status.

4. **Payroll runs list**: A list page with status filter tabs, pagination, and links to each run's detail page.

5. **Run detail**: A detail page showing the uploaded document alongside a journal entries table with columns for account code, account name, department, debit, credit, and memo. Show totals at the bottom and highlight if out of balance. Post and reject buttons when in review status.

6. **GL accounts**: A collection of payroll GL accounts (code, name, account type) manageable from a settings page.

7. **Seed data**: A seed script with sample GL accounts and a couple of demo payroll runs with journal entries.

templates.name: fixed-asset-tracker
templates.title: Fixed Asset Tracker
templates.description: Track fixed assets, calculate depreciation, and manage disposals with automated journal entries.
templates.starter_prompt:
Build me a fixed asset tracking app. I want to maintain an asset register, run monthly depreciation calculations, view depreciation schedules, and handle asset disposals with gain/loss tracking.

Here is what I need:

1. **Asset register**: A collection of fixed assets with fields for name, asset tag, category (equipment, furniture, vehicles, software, buildings, leasehold improvements), status (active, fully depreciated, disposed), acquisition date, cost basis, salvage value, useful life in months, depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance), accumulated depreciation, location, department, and disposal fields.

2. **Dashboard**: A dashboard showing net book value across all assets, total asset count (with active count), accumulated depreciation, and fully depreciated count. Include a category breakdown showing NBV and count per category, and a depreciation progress visualization.

3. **Asset list**: A list page with status and category filters, pagination, and columns for name, category, cost basis, net book value, and status.

4. **Asset detail**: A detail page with financial summary cards (cost, accumulated depreciation, NBV, depreciation %), asset info, an edit form, depreciation schedule table showing each period's entry, and buttons to run depreciation or dispose the asset.

5. **Depreciation automation**: A Python automation that calculates monthly depreciation for a given period. It should support both straight-line and declining-balance methods, create depreciation entry records, update accumulated depreciation on the asset, and auto-set status to "fully depreciated" when NBV reaches salvage value. Support running for a single asset or all active assets at once.

6. **Disposal**: A way to dispose an asset — record disposal date and proceeds, calculate gain/loss, and set status to disposed.

7. **GL accounts**: A collection of asset GL accounts manageable from a settings page.

8. **Seed data**: A seed script with sample assets across different categories, useful lives, and acquisition dates, plus some depreciation history.
